title: solidago speciosa subsp. pallida
intro: Solidago speciosa subsp. pallida, commonly known as pale goldenrod, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the aster family that is native to eastern North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of pale goldenrod include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Pale goldenrod can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine and natural health practices.
